title: Automation to set timeline dates based on status changes
excerpt:
I would like to see the ability to change a timeline end date upon a status change. 
If a project is completed prior to the end of the expected timeline then you should be able to adjust the status to “done” and the time…

posts.author: lbalchunas (Luke Balchunas)
posts.cookedHtml:
I would like to see the ability to change a timeline end date upon a status change.

If a project is completed prior to the end of the expected timeline then you should be able to adjust the status to “done” and the timeline can update automatically.

posts.author: basdebruin (Bas de Bruin)
posts.cookedHtml:
hi @lbalchunas

Welcome to the community! It is possible to set a date to today when a status changes. With the app “Start + End = Timeline” two dates (start and end) can be sync to a timeline. When combining these two in one automation you can achieve what you want.

image1655×171 20.2 KB

posts.author: koko03_30 (Konstantin Botev)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi, Luke,

I think that the TImeline Manipulator app will be pretty helpful in your case. You can check here how it works:

auth.monday.com

Timeline manipulator - monday.com Marketplace

Automations trigger timeline updates when column values change.

Best of luck,
Konstantin
